def: s.f. = H اندهياو अंधियाव andhiyāʼo [अन्धी+S. वातः], s.m. Dust-storm, whirlwind; storm, tempest, hurricane, cyclone
subs: āṅdhī uṭhnā, āṅdhī ćalnā, lit. 'A storm to come on, or to arise, or to be raging'; to blow hard, to blow a hurricane
lit. 'Mangoes blown off in a storm'; windfall; things obtained cheap
To prove a veritable tempest; to be very active and ardent; to engage keenly and ardently (in).

def: s.m. = H آندهي आंधी āṅdhī [S. अन्ध+इका], s.f. Dust-storm, whirlwind; storm, tempest, hurricane, cyclone
subs: āṅdhī uṭhnā, āṅdhī ćalnā, lit. 'A storm to come on, or to arise, or to be raging'; to blow hard, to blow a hurricane
lit. 'Mangoes blown off in a storm'; windfall; things obtained cheap
To prove a veritable tempest; to be very active and ardent; to engage keenly and ardently (in).

def: adj. & s.m. = andherā, q.v.
adj. & s.f. = andherī, q.v.
s.m. see s.v. andh.
adj. (f. -ī), Blind; dark, &c. ( = andhā, q.v.); purblind, shortsighted.
s.m. = andhāpan, q.v.

ety: S. अन्+धौतः; the Sanskrit form is a-dhauta
def: part. adj. Unwashed, unclean.
= an-dhoʼā, q.v.
adj. = andhlā, q.v.
s.f. = andhā-pan, q.v.
s.f. A kind of cloth or garment.

ety: contrac. from aranḍī, q.v. and c.f. anḍ
def: s.f. The fruit of the Palma Christi or Ricinus communis
v.n. To be sitting on eggs, or to have young (a bird); — v.t. To prick the testicles of a bullock in order to make him go faster.
(corrupt) = andha-kār, q.v.
s.m. Grief, anxiety, trouble
subs: andoh-nāk, adj. Full of grief, sorrowful, afflicted, sad, mournfur.

ety: perf. part. of andoḵẖtan, Zend ham+rt. dug; S. सम्+दुह्
def: part. Gained, acquired; — s.m. Acquisitions, gain, profit.
v.n. To swing.
(fr. andoḵẖtan; see andoḵẖta), part. act. Acquiring, gaining (used as last member of compounds).
= adrakī, q.v.
= a-droh, q.v.
s.m. The chain or rope with which the feet of elephants are tied; chain or ring worn on the ankle (commonly by wrestlers); anklet.
adj. Possessed of testicles, entire; having large testicles; — s.m. Bull, entire ox.
adj. Possessed of testicles, entire; having large testicles; — s.m. Bull, entire ox.

ety: = andūda, perf. part. of andūdan; Zend ham+rt. dā; S. sam+dhā
def: part. Covered, overlaid, incrusted, plastered over, washed over; anointed, smeared, &c. (used as last member of compounds, e.g. zar-andūd, covered or overlaid with gold, gilded).
adj. & s.m. = āṅḍū.
s.f. A hen or other fowl that is on the point of laying.
adj. Not one, many, manifold, several, diverse, various; much, abundant; (in Gram.) plural
subs: adj. Multiform; of various kinds or sorts, of variable mind, fickle
s.m. A person who is a member of several gotras or families

def: = albelā, q.v.
s.m. Simplicity, &c. = albelāpan, q.v.
s.m. The time allotted for agricultural labour in the hot season (viz. sunrise till noon).
s.m. pl. (of nafar), Individuals, persons; soldiers, servants.
s.m. pl. (of nafas), Breathings, respirations; breaths, voices, words; spirits (perhaps mistakenly, as though it were the plural of nafs; see next).
s.m. pl. (of nafs), Spirits, souls, minds; animate things; persons.
s.m. Body, limb, member; part, portion, share; a division or subdivision (of a science, &c.); — āṅg, s.m. Demand per head for pasturing cattle, an agistment
subs: (anga-vidyā), s.f. The science which treats of the structure of the body, anatomy ( = ʻilm-ě-tashrīḥ); knowledge of the lucky and unlucky marks of the body
(-kā), To cut off a limb; to mutilate
(-kā), To have a limb cut off; to be mutilated; to be crippled; to be stiff in the joints
(or āṅg) pakaṛnā, To gain flesh, become plump or fat
angaj, s.m. lit. 'Produced from the body'; a son; — anga-jā, ang-jāʼī, s.f. A daughter
s.m. Cutting off a limb; mutilation
s.m. Application of scented unguents or cosmetics to the body (especially after bathing)
s.f. Shivering of the body; ague
(or āṅg) godnā (-kā), To puncture the body and introduce colouring matter (generally indigo), to tattoo

(or āṅg) lagānā or lagā-denā (-meṅ), To place (a thing) close to the body, to place the body close (to a thing); to embrace, clasp; to put on, to wear (clothes); to carry or take away (with one); to unite or join (-to, -ke, as in marriage)
(or āṅg) lagnā, v.n. To be brought or placed in contact with the body; to be embraced; to be assimilated (food); to be nourished, grow stout; to tell (upon)

(or āṅg) moṛnā, To turn or twist the body, to stretch (oneself); to turn away (from, -se), shrink (from); to avoid, shun; to bend or bow (in humility), to be humble and unassuming; — āṅg-moṛī, s.f. Stretching; yawning
s.m. A religious ceremony which consists in touching certain parts of the body according to the rules prescribed in the Śāstras
s.m. The proprietor of a small portion of a village; mutual aid in tillage.

def: s.m. A long coat or tunic worn by men (Hindūs and Mohammadans).
s.f. Nurse, nursemaid, āyā.
s.m. As much as can be embraced in both arms, a double armful (syn. kaulī); — axle, axle-tree (of a cart); hire, fare.
s.m. Body, limb, member; part, portion, share; a division or subdivision (of a science, &c.); — āṅg, s.m. Demand per head for pasturing cattle, an agistment
subs: (anga-vidyā), s.f. The science which treats of the structure of the body, anatomy ( = ʻilm-ě-tashrīḥ); knowledge of the lucky and unlucky marks of the body

(-kā), To have a limb cut off; to be mutilated; to be crippled; to be stiff in the joints

angaj, s.m. lit. 'Produced from the body'; a son; — anga-jā, ang-jāʼī, s.f. A daughter

s.m. Application of scented unguents or cosmetics to the body (especially after bathing)
s.f. Shivering of the body; ague
(or āṅg) godnā (-kā), To puncture the body and introduce colouring matter (generally indigo), to tattoo

(or āṅg) lagānā or lagā-denā (-meṅ), To place (a thing) close to the body, to place the body close (to a thing); to embrace, clasp; to put on, to wear (clothes); to carry or take away (with one); to unite or join (-to, -ke, as in marriage)
(or āṅg) lagnā, v.n. To be brought or placed in contact with the body; to be embraced; to be assimilated (food); to be nourished, grow stout; to tell (upon)

s.m. A religious ceremony which consists in touching certain parts of the body according to the rules prescribed in the Śāstras
s.m. The proprietor of a small portion of a village; mutual aid in tillage.

def: s.m. Honey ( = anjabīn).
s.m. A bracelet worn on the upper arm; (in Myth.) Name of the son of Bālī, king of the monkeys.
s.m. One who carries money or jewels about his person, concealed in his quilted coat.
s.f. Small cakes of flour, &c. baked on live coals or cinders (cf. ćapātī).
s.f. Small cakes of flour, &c. baked on live coals or cinders (cf. ćapātī).
s.m. Inclosed space adjoining a house; court-yard, court, area, quadrangle (also written angnā and angnāʼī: syn. ṣaḥn and ćauk).
wety: Inherited from Prakrit 𑀅𑀁𑀕𑀡 (aṃgaṇa), from Sanskrit अङ्गन (aṅgana).

def: s.f. A well-formed woman, a beauty; a woman.
adj. = an-ginā, q.v.
adj. = an-ginā, q.v.
s.m. Heated charcoal, a live coal, fire, firebrand, a bit of fire, cinder, spark; burning matter; charcoal; Tuesday
subs: (or angāroṅ-par) loṭnā, To roll on fire (as a religious austerity); to be very restless or disquieted, to lie (as it were) on thorns; to burn with rage, envy, or jealousy
To rain sparks of fire, to rain fire

def: s.m. Heated charcoal, a live coal, fire, firebrand, a bit of fire, cinder, spark; burning matter; charcoal; Tuesday
subs: (or angāroṅ-par) loṭnā, To roll on fire (as a religious austerity); to be very restless or disquieted, to lie (as it were) on thorns; to burn with rage, envy, or jealousy
To rain sparks of fire, to rain fire

def: adj. = an-gaṛhā, q.v.

ety: S. अन्+घटित; cf. the H. an-ghar
def: adj. Unformed, unfashioned, unshaped, unworked, unwrought, unfinished, crude, rough, unpolished, uncivilized, ill-bred, ill-mannered, rude, uncouth, awkward, clownish; unset (as a stone or jewel); ill-arranged, ill-matched; disconnected, incoherent; wild, absurd (as speech, &c.); — s.m. A rough, awkward fellow; an unlicked cub. (See anghaṛ.)

ety: S. अन्+घटित; cf. the H. an-ghar
def: adj. Unformed, unfashioned, unshaped, unworked, unwrought, unfinished, crude, rough, unpolished, uncivilized, ill-bred, ill-mannered, rude, uncouth, awkward, clownish; unset (as a stone or jewel); ill-arranged, ill-matched; disconnected, incoherent; wild, absurd (as speech, &c.); — s.m. A rough, awkward fellow; an unlicked cub. (See anghaṛ.)
s.f. A small fire-pan; chafing-dish, brazier, a small stove (syn. angeṭhī).
s.f. dim. of angārā, q.v.
s.m. lit. 'Body protector'; a long tunic or coat worn by men (Musalmans and Hindūs, — the former having it made to open on the left (cf. ćap-kan), the latter to open on the right: — syn. angā).
s.f. dim. of angarkhā, q.v.
s.m. Perquisites from the threshing-floor to the village god, Brāhmans, &c.
s.m. Allowing the use of a plough in lieu of wages in money or kind.
s.f. = angleṭ, q.v.

ety: of angeḵẖtan; Zend hãm+rt. vij; S. sam+vij
def: adj. & s.m. Strife-exciting; mischievous; mischief-maker, &c.).
v.t. To stir up, excite; to bear, endure, suffer, experience.
= aghānā, q.v.
adj. = an-gaṛh, q.v.
s.f. Disconnected, or incoherent, or nonsensical speech; nonsense, gibberish.
s.m. Roughness, crudeness; awkwardness, clumsiness, want of polish, coarseness, rudeness, incivility, ill-breeding, bad manners; stupidity.
s.m. Roughness, crudeness; awkwardness, clumsiness, want of polish, coarseness, rudeness, incivility, ill-breeding, bad manners; stupidity.
s.f. Unsuitable time, inopportune moment.
s.m. A ring of mixed metal worn on the great toe by people of low caste.
adj. Bodily, corporeal.
s.f. A short under-jacket worn by women; bodice; stays
subs: s.f. The receptacles for the breasts in a bodice; gussets.

def: s.m. Agreement, acquiescence, assent, consent; acceptance, reception; promise, submission; avowal, acknowledgment, confession; selection, choice
subs: To agree to, acquiesce in, accept; to admit, confess, avow, acknowledge (syn. qabūl karnā); to concede; to promise; to submit to, to obey; to undertake.

def: part. Agreed to, accepted, &c. (see angīkār).
adj. Uncounted, unreckoned, untold; countless, numberless, innumerable, incalculable ( = be-gintī, be-shumār)
subs: s.m. lit. 'The unreckoned year'; the eighth year of one's age (cf. next)
s.m. lit. 'The unreckoned month'; the eighth month of pregnancy. (As an eight month's child seldom lives, the eighth month or year has come to be superstitiously regarded in the East as unlucky.)

def: s.m. Receptacle for fire, fire-stand; a goldsmith's furnace.
s.f. A vessel for holding fire, brazier, chafing-dish, grate, stove.
s.f. A short under-jacket worn by women; bodice; stays
subs: s.f. The receptacles for the breasts in a bodice; gussets.

def: s.f. Body, person, form, build, make, physical frame.
s.m. = āṅgan, q.v.
s.f. Court-yard, &c. = āṅgan, q.v.

ety: S. अङ्ग+वस्त्र, or ang+poćhā, fr. poćhnā, 'to wipe,' q.v.
def: s.m. A cloth which Hindūs fasten round their waists when bathing, and afterwards wipe themselves with; a towel.
s.f. dim. of angoćhā, q.v.
s.m. Gnat, musquito, midge.
s.f. = angleṭ, q.v.
s.f. = انگڙائی angṛāʼī, q.v.
s.f. Twisting or stretching the limbs (especially the upper members); yawning
subs: To stretch oneself, or to yawn, so as to impart the desire to do likewise to another (which is disapproved of)
To stretch the limbs, to endeavour to shake off drowsiness; to yawn ( = angṛānā).

ety: caus. from S. अग् or अङ्ग्
def: v.n. To twist the body, stretch the limbs; to yawn; to roll and toss about in bed (see the more common agṛāʼī lenā, s.v. angṛāʼī).
s.m. An Englishman.
An Englishwoman.
adj. English, British; — s.f. English, the English language = angrezī-zabān
subs: s.m. A student of English, one who is studying English
s.m. One who knows English; an English scholar.

def: s.f. Armour; doublet.
= angūṭhā, q.v.
s.m. Finger; thumb; great toe; finger's length; finger's breadth, a measure equal to eight barley-corns
s.f. Finger, finger's breadth
subs: To raise the finger; to point the finger (at); to make a sign; to stir; — unglī-par (or ungliyoṅ-par) naćānā (-ko), lit. 'To make one dance upon, or after, one's fingers'; to lead (one) a fine dance, to harass, jade, distress; to do as one pleases with (a person); to trifle with, play with, make a fool of, make jest of, ridicule
v.n. To snap or crack the fingers
(-par), To place the finger (upon); to cover or conceal with a finger; to point out (errors), to criticize, find fault (with); to interfere (with), molest, worry, harass
(-ko), To touch with a finger, lay a finger upon, to strike
To jeer, taunt, or annoy one by moving the middle finger (before him)
lit. 'Fingers to rise'; to be notorious, to be the object of scorn or contempt ( = angusht-numā honā)
s.f. The finger with which sandal is applied or sprinkled; the finger on which the wedding ring is worn by European women
(or denā), To place the fore-finger between the teeth by way of expressing astonishment, sorrow, or amazement at a sudden calamity; to be astonished or amazed
s.f. The middle-finger ( = bīć-kī, or bićlī unglī: It is so called from the superstitious notion that the finger has some bewitching or malignant virtue in it)

def: s.m. The Grape, Vitis vinifera; granulations in a healing sore, cicatrice, scab of a sore
subs: angūr bandhnā, angūr bhar-lānā, v.n. To cicatrize, to heal (as a sore)
angūr taṛakhnā, v.n. The cicatrice or scab of a sore or wound to crack or break
s.m. = angūr-kī ṭaṭṭī, s.f. Vine-arbour or frame, vinery, vineyard; fireworks in imitation of clusters on a vine.

def: s.f. = angulī, unglī, q.v.
adj. Of or belonging to the grape

ety: Pehl. angust, Zend angusta, S. अङ्गुष्ठा
def: s.f. Finger
subs: s.f. The fore-finger (lit. 'the finger of testimony or profession,' — so called because the Mohammadans hold it up when making a profession of faith

adj. Pointed at with the finger; famous, notorious; pointed at in scorn, held in contempt; — s.m. An object of distinction or notoriety; an object of scorn or contempt; — angusht-numā honā, To be pointed at or out as a famous person, to be famous; to be pointed to in scorn, be made the object of scorn, &c.

def: s.m. A ring, — especially one worn on the thumb; an archer's thumb-stall; a thimble.
s.f. A ring, — especially one worn on the finger (in contradistinction to angushtāna: syn. angūṭhī.
s.m. Thumb; great toe; thumb's breadth (as a measure: See angūṭhā).
s.m. Thumb; great toe
subs: (-kā), lit. 'To kiss the toe'; to flatter, act the toady (to), play the sycophant
(-ko), To show the thumb (to one) in defiance or derision, or to signify a desire for sexual intercourse; to trifle with; to signify 'Don't you wish you may get it' (cf. ṭhengā dikhānā)
(-ko), To regard as vile or contemptible, to hold of no account.

def: s.f. Finger-ring; toe-ring.
s.m. Assafœtida.
s.m. See s.v. ang.
s.m. Bathing.
v.n. To bathe.
s.f. plur. (of nahr), Streams, rivers, rivulets, streamlets.
v.t. To carry off, filch, pilfer.
adj. Without affection, unkind; unfriendly, inimical; — s.m. Enemy; — want of affection, coldness; enmity.
adj. & s.m. = an-hit, q.v.
adj. Not to be; not likely to be; impossible; unlikely, improbable; — unpromising, hopeless; — an-honī, s.f. That which is not to be, or which is not likely to be; impossibility; improbability.
adj. = an-honā and an-honī, q.v.
adj. Not to be; not likely to be; impossible; unlikely, improbable; — unpromising, hopeless; — an-honī, s.f. That which is not to be, or which is not likely to be; impossibility; improbability.

ety: an+hotā, perf. part. of honā, q.v.
def: s.f. Want, poverty, indigence.

ety: an+hotā, perf. part. of honā, q.v.
def: s.f. Want, poverty, indigence.
s.f. = an-honī, q.v.
v.t. To bathe, cause to bathe, to wash.
s.m. Point or edge of a sharp instrument or weapon. (See aṇī.)
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀅𑀡- (aṇa-), from Sanskrit अन्- (an-), from Proto-Indo-European *n̥- (“not, un-”).

def: s.f. Point (of a spear, arrow &c.), end, tip; face, front; stem or prow (of a boat)
s.f. Absence of desire, disinclination, unwillingness; indifference.
adj. Awake; wakeful, sleepless.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian اندر (andar, “inside, within”).

def: adj. Listless, apathetic; indifferent.
s.f. Face, front; edge, point; army, forces, host (see anī).
v.n. See s.v. ānā.
s.f. Army, a military force consisting of one-tenth of an akshauhinī, q.v. (cf. anīk).
s.f. (but m. in S.), Wind, air; breath.
wety: Learned borrowing from Sanskrit अनल m (anala, “fire”).

def: s.m. The superhuman faculty of making oneself infinitesimally small; the subtle and invisible state that can be assumed by a god, or which is supposed to be attainable by austere devotion or by magical arts.
s.m. = aṇi-mā, q.v.
adj. Blameless, irreproachable.
adj. Unskilled, unskilful.
adj. Not clear; not pure; dirty, foul.
s.m. Uncertainty.
part. adj. Unobstructed, unrestrained.
adj. Not fit to be spoken; incapable of being uttered or expressed in words, indescribable, indefinable.
adj. Not fit to be spoken; incapable of being uttered or expressed in words, indescribable, indefinable.
adj. Not fit to be spoken; incapable of being uttered or expressed in words, indescribable, indefinable.
s.m. lit. 'Not God'; created existence.
s.m. Companion, familiar
adj. Uncertain, doubtful; — s.m. Uncertainty, doubt.
adj. Unprohibited, unforbidden.
part. adj. Undesire ; undesirable; deprecable; bad, evil; ominous.
s.f. Unsteadiastness, unsteadiness.
s.m. Aniso seed, Pimpinella anisum.
adj. Not lasting for ever, frail, perishable, transient, fleeting.
s.f. Injustice, wrong; outrage; oppression; iniquity, wickedness, immorality; impropriety, unmannerliness, rudeness; disturbance, commotion
subs: To raise a disturbance, cause a commotion, perform lawless acts

def: s.f. Injustice, wrong; outrage; oppression; iniquity, wickedness, immorality; impropriety, unmannerliness, rudeness; disturbance, commotion
subs: To raise a disturbance, cause a commotion, perform lawless acts

def: adj. Uneternal, not lasting or permanent, temporary, frail, perishable, fleeting, transient ( = anit)
subs: anitya-dattaka, adj. Temporarily granted; — s.m. A child adopted for a certain term.

def: s.f. Want of permanency, transient existence, transitoriness, frailness.
adj. Unchecked, unimpeded, unopposed.
adj. Not to be warded off; irresistible.
s.m. A creeping plant, Polygonum bistorta, from which a drink is prepared with sugar for obstinate colds and hemorrhages. (The plant is brought from the banks of the Euphrates.)
= P. angabīn (of which it is the Arabicized form).
s.m. A mongrel or hybrid animal.
s.m. = anćal, q.v.
s.m. The open hands joined on the ulnar side, with the palms upward and hollowed so as to hold or support anything; the hollow formed by so joining the hands
s.m. The open hands joined on the ulnar side, with the palms upward and hollowed so as to hold or support anything; the hollow formed by so joining the hands
adj. (f. -ī), Unburnt, unscorched.

s.f. = anjlā, q.v.; — as much of anything as can be contained in the hands placed side by side with the palms hollowed; a mode of salutation, or of supplication, by carrying the hands placed side by side (as described under anjlā) to the forehead.

ety: Zend antāma, S. antima, or an+rt. Zend jam = S. gam
def: s.m. End, termination, completion, accomplishment, conclusion; result, upshot; accident; vexation
subs: v.n. To be finished, terminated, accomplished, &c. anjām denā, v.t. To bring to a termination, to accomplish, to wind up; to fulfil, carry out, comply with, discharge, manage
anjām-kār, adv. The end of the business, at last, finally
anjām-ko pahuṅćānā, v.t. = anjām denā, q.v.
v.n. To be at an end, to come or draw to an end, to end, cease, stop; to ensue.
ex: اس کا انجام ٹھیک نہیں ہو گا۔
is kā añjām ṭhīk nahī̃ ho gā
The result of it won't be right.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian انجام (anjām), from Middle Persian [script needed] (hanǰām, “end, completion, outcome”).

ety: S. अन्+जन्म+कः; and cf. H. jamnā
def: adj. Barren, unproductive (soil); syn. banjar.
s.f. An inferior kind of rice; a species of grass, Memecylon tinctorum. (It grows in great abundance in the North-west Provinces, and is used as fodder.)
s.m. Collyrium, ointment, lamp-black, antimony, black pigment or collyrium applied to the eyes and eyelashes to embellish them. (It is also used for weak sight or pain in the eye: syn. kuḥl, surma)
subs: adj. Tinted or painted with collyrium
s.f. A stye in the eye; — an insect ( = kumbār).

def: s.m. Collyrium, ointment, lamp-black, antimony, black pigment or collyrium applied to the eyes and eyelashes to embellish them. (It is also used for weak sight or pain in the eye: syn. kuḥl, surma)
subs: adj. Tinted or painted with collyrium
s.f. A stye in the eye; — an insect ( = kumbār).

def: adj. ( = ajān), Unknown; not knowing, ignorant, stupid, foolish; unwitting, unintentional; — s.m. An unknown person, a stranger; an ignorant person
subs: v.n. To pretend or affect ignorance, to assume the stranger
adv. In ignorance, ignorantly, &c. = an-jāne, q.v.

def: s.m. Engine, steam-engine.
adj. ( = ajān), Unknown; not knowing, ignorant, stupid, foolish; unwitting, unintentional; — s.m. An unknown person, a stranger; an ignorant person
subs: v.n. To pretend or affect ignorance, to assume the stranger
adv. In ignorance, ignorantly, &c. = an-jāne, q.v.
ex: کسی انجانی سچائی کے لیے یہ جنگ لڑا گیا ہے۔
kisī anjānī sacāī ke liye ye jang laṛā gayā hai.
This war has been fought for some unknown truth.

def: adj. Unborn.
s.m. One of the names of Hanumān.
s.f. v.s.v. anjan, 'collyrium.'
s.f. Ignorance, stupidity; negligence.
s.f. A woman whose eyes are painted with collyrium, or who is perfumed with sandal, &c.; — a certain medicinal plant used as a sedative and laxative.
s.f. An epithet of the mother of Hanumān (S. anjanā); a stye on the eyelid; a species of small lizard, the house-lizard.
s.f. A kind of newt or lizard.
s.m. A nettle.
s.f. = anjlā, and aṅjlī, q.q.v.
s.m. Disrepute, disgrace, infamy.
s.m. Interval; postponement, adjournment
subs: An interval, postponement, &c. to take place.

def: s.m. = āṅsū, q.v.
s.f. Selvage of a piece of cloth; hem of a handkerchief, &c. (cf. anćal).
s.m. A kind of small lizard. (See anjanī.)
s.m. The fig, Ficus carica
s.f. A species of grass, &c. ( = anjan, q.v.).
v.t. To apply collyrium or lamp-black, &c. to the eyes. (Syn. sārnā, surma lagānā.)
v.t. To apply collyrium or lamp-black, &c. to the eyes. (Syn. sārnā, surma lagānā.)
s.m. = آنسو āṅsū, q.v.
s.m. pl. (of najm), Stars.

ety: Pehl. hanjuman, Zend hanjamana, S. सङ्गमा
def: s.f. Assembly, meeting, company, society, institution; party, banquet.
ex: اس انجمن میں آپ کو آنا ہے بار بار
is anjuman mẽ āp ko ānā hai bār bār
You will have to come again and again to this gathering
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian انجمن (anjuman, “meeting, assembly”). First attested in c. 1564 as Middle Hindi انجمن (anjmn /⁠anjuman⁠/).

def: s.f. The science of numbers, arithmetic (syn. ʻilm-ě-ḥisāb)
s.f. The science of numbers, arithmetic (syn. ʻilm-ě-ḥisāb)

ety: fem. of aʻnaq, 'long-necked'; rt. عنق 'to be long in the neck'
def: s.m. A fabulous bird, the phœnix; a rara avis (syn. sīmurg̠); — adj. Scarce, rare, hard to get or find; wonderful, curious
subs: To be rare, &c. (e.g. is zamāne-meṅ rozgār ʻanqā hai).

def: s.f. A spider.
s.m. Arithmetician; accountant.
v.t. To cause to be valued, to have or get (a thing) valued or appraised; to value, appraise; ( = āṅknā, q.v.); to examine (as cloth); to approve of.
s.m. Valuation, appraisement.
s.m. = āṅk, q.v.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian اِنْکَار (inkār), borrowed from Arabic إِنْكَار (ʔinkār), from أَنْكَرَ (ʔankara).

def: adj. compar. & superl. (of نکير nakīr), More, or most, offensive; very disagreeable or unpleasant; odious, detestable.
adj. & adv. See s.v. ʻan.
v.n. To curl up (through heat, &c.); to shrink, contract; to become crisp or dry (cf akaṛnā).
s.m. = āk, q.v. (syn. madār).
s.f. Eye; sight; look, glance, regard, observation; discernment, judgment, sense
subs: To have inflammation of the eyes, to suffer from ophthalmia; to have a sore eye; to be blear-eyed
s.f. A stye on the eyelid (see anjanī and anjanhārī)

To raise the eyes; to give up, leave off, relinquish, resign; — āṅkh uṭhākar na dekhnā (-ko), lit. 'Not to raise the eyes and look'; not to notice, to disregard, to treat with disdain; to keep the eyes down; to be abashed or ashamed; to be humble, bashful, shy, or diffident
v.n. To be unable to raise the eyes (through shame, &c.)

(-kī), To avoid the eye (of), elude the observation (of); to steal away secretly, slink away; — to avoid seeing or noticing, to pretend not to see (one); to turn the eyes aside, look another way; — āṅkh baćā-ke, adv. Secretly, stealthily
v.n. To take off the eyes; to have (one's) attention withdrawn
(or āṅkheṅ) badalnā or badal-lenā, To regard with altered looks; to view with variable regards; to be fickle or inconstant; to be changed; to withdraw (one's) favour or regard
(apnī), To preserve (one's) look or regard unaltered, to keep friendship or regard unaltered
Not to be able to look steadfastly into another's face; to be ashamed, shy, or sheepish
(-se, or -kī t̤araf-se), To shut the eyes (to); to close the eyes, fall asleep, die; to turn away (from), take no notice (of), to treat with neglect; to retire (from), relinquish, give up
v.n. lit. 'The eyes to be closed'; to be asleep; to fall asleep; to die
āṅkh bhar or bhar-ke dekhnā or dekh-lenā

(-kī t̤araf), To look (at) till (one's) curiosity is fully satisfied; to stare steadily and long (at); to bestow a full gaze (upon), to cast angry looks (at), look angry or threatening (at); — to cast amorous glances (at), gaze amorously (upon)
(or ānkheṅ) bhar-lānā, To have eyes full of tears, to be ready to cry

āṅkh-ṭeṛhī karnā, To raise the eyes and eyebrows (in anger, &c.), to look angry, to frown; to look disgusted, or with dislike (at), make a wry face (at); to show contempt, look disdainfully (upon or at)
(or āṅkheṅ) pathrānā or pathrā-jānā, The eyes to become petrified, the eyes to become dim through constant gazing, &c.)
To look or gaze (at) with strained and eager eyes, to observe with eagerness and anxiety; to stare (at) with all (one's) eyes; to look (at) in astonishment; to scrutinize closely
To feel a pulsation in the eye (if the pulsation be in the right eye of a man or the left of a woman it is regarded as an omen of some desirable event, whilst the contrary is considered unlucky)

lit. 'The eye to burst and run out'; to lose an eye, to become blind; to have one's eyes knocked out or put out; — āṅkh phūṭī pīr gaʼī, prov. 'The eye is lost and the pain is gone'; better the eye out than constant pain (said also of a contention which has ceased through the object of it being lost to both parties)
āṅkh-phoṛ ṭiḍḍā, s.m. A midge that flies into the eyes at night
(-kī), To destroy the eye (of), to render blind; to ruin the sight (by hard study, eager watching, &c.); to study hard, apply (oneself) closely; to watch or expect in vain
(-se), To look another way, turn away (from), avoid, shun; to turn up the eye (as in pain, &c.)
To open the eyes wide, to stare; to look astonished; to be discerning, wise, prudent, or judicious

To cool the eyes with water (when heated with weeping, as is the Hindū custom on the third day after the death of a relative); to refresh the eye, to gratify the eye (with a sight of); to derive consolation, be comforted
lit. 'The eyes to blink'; to blink; to doze, be heavy with sleep, begin to fall asleep; to be dazzled; to be timid
lit. 'The eyes to lower or close'; to doze, &c. ( = āṅkh jhapaknā, q.v.); to have the eyes lowered (through modesty, shame, or timidity

(-se), To avert the eyes (from, — through pride, shame, or dislike); to avoid the eyes or sight (of); to avoid, shun; not to attend (to), not to notice, to disregard; to pretend not to see, to connive (at)
To look angry, &c. ( = āṅkh-bhauṅ ćaṛhānā, q.v.); to be intoxicated (see next)
(-se), To have eyes heavy or drowsy (from intoxication, or want of sleep, or head-ache); to have eyes marked by debauch; to have the eyes turned up (in death), to be dying
To make the eyes fla h or dance; to roll one's eyes (in anger, or as a blandishment)
āṅkh ćīr-ke dekhnā = āṅkh phāṛ-phāṛ-ke dekhnā, q.v.
To close, or press close, the eyes; to forbid by signs
To look angry or threatening, to stare defiantly; to frown, scowl ( = āṅkh-bhauṅ ćaṛhānā and āṅkh ćaṛhānā); to menace, brow-beat, deter
(-kī), To observe the eyes (of) with a view to guidance, &c.; to receive education in the company (of)
adv. After having observed (another's) eyes, at a hint or sign (from)
(-par), To cast or set eyes (on), to look (over), glance (at); to cast wanton glances (at)
To have the eyes full of tears ( = āṅkh bhar lānā)
(obsolete), lit. 'To have the eyes covered'; to die; to be ashamed
(-par), To have the eyes fixed (on), to look hopefully (to); to regard with love or friendship; to set lustful or wanton eyes (upon = āṅkh ḍālnā)
lit. 'To redden the eyes'; to look angry, to be very angry: āṅkh-se dekhke, adv. With the eyes open, with full knowledge (of consequences); knowingly, wittingly
(-se), To rejoice or delight the eyes (with a sight of); to meet or to visit a friend or a person of rank
(-kī), To fall in the regard or esteem (of), to lose the favour or regard (of), to be out of favour, be disgraced, become contemptible
lit. 'To warm the eyes'; to refresh, rejoice, or feast the eyes (with the sight of); to derive consolation (from), to comfort or console oneself
lit. 'The pupil of the eye'; = āṅkh-kī putlī, q.v.
To have a painful throbbing in the eye, have a pain in the eyes

(-kī), lit. 'The eyes to open'; to be born; to awake from sleep, become awake; to have the eyes opened; to become aware (of), to attain to a knowledge (of); to become conscious; to be alive (to), to be put on (one's) guard (against); to be astonished or amazed

(-kī), To open the eyes (of); to make aware or cognizant (of), to impart knowledge (to); to open (one's own) eyes, to awake from sleep, to open the eyes wide, to stare; to stare in wonder, astonishment, &c.; to become wise, discerning, or prudent
(or āṅkhoṅ-) kī putlī, lit. 'The pupil of the eye'; the apple of (one's) eye, darling, pet
āṅkh-ke sāmhne, adv. In the sight (of), before the eyes (of), in presence (of)
To have the eyes lowered through shame or modesty ( = āṅkh jhuknā)
(-se), To look steadfastly (at), meet stare with stare; to interchange glances; to communicate a secret by signs; to cast amorous glances, make love with the eyes ( = āṅkh mi-lānā)
(-se), To encounter the eyes (of); to exchange love-glances; to fall in love (with), be enamoured (of)
(-se), To fix the eyes (on), form an attachment (to), fall in love (with); to look up (to), to respect, honour

lit. 'The eyes to close'; to fall asleep, to doze; to have the eyes fixed on another (as an object of affection), to be enamoured (of); — āṅkh-lagā, s.m. A man on whom a woman's affections are set, a paramour; — āṅkh-lagī, s.f. A woman on whom a man's affections are set; a mistress
To make a sign with the eye, to wink, tip the wink; stop (any one) by a sign; to cast amorous glances
lit. 'To shut and open the eyes alternately'; to blink; to wink, make a sign
(or mućauwal), s.m. āṅkh-mićaulā (or -mućaulā), s.m. āṅkh mićaulī (or -mućaulī), s.f. Blind man's buff (syn. ćashm-bandak; — see āṅkh mūćnā)
To look (one) in the face, gaze full in the face (of); to exchange glances (see āṅkh laṛānā); to contract friendship, &c.
(or -mić-) jānā, lit. 'The eye to become closed'; to die
āṅkh-muṅdauwal, s.m. Blind man's buff ( = āṅkh mićaulī)
adv. With the eyes shut, blindly, inconsiderately
or mūćnā, To close the eyes, to blindfold; to shut and close the eyes, to wink ( = āṅkh mićkānā); to wink (at), connive (at)
To look one boldly in the face, meet glance with glance (after the manner of one who is not ashamed, or has nothing to fear)
āṅkh-meṅ jī ānā, aṅkh-meṅ dam ānā, To have fresh life in the eyes, to revive, recover; — to be reduced to great straits, be reduced to the last extremity, be at the point of death
lit. 'To have no moisture in the eyes'; to be cold and indifferent, to be hard-hearted or pitiless; to be bold, brazen, shameless
(-par, or -ke sāmhne), To be unable to fix the gaze (on); to be dazzled (by)
To have no eyes, not to look; to lack discernment, be mentally blind; — to have no hope or expectation
āṅkhoṅ-par rakhnā, v.t. lit. 'To set or place on one's eyes'; to treat with esteem, veneration, or honour; to prize, hold dear, love, regard highly
To be revered or esteemed; to be beloved; to be treated with honour
To be blind to all shame, to be shameless; to be wanting in feeling, to care nothing (for), to be indifferent (to)
āṅkhoṅ-se dekhnā, To see with one's own eyes
kānoṅ sunā na mānā, prov. Seeing is believing
adv. With all my heart and soul, willingly, cheerfully ( = ba sar o ćashm)
To draw tears from the eyes; to try the eyes sorely (by close application), to pore over (a book), work hard (with the needle, &c.)
lit. 'To steal the lamp-black of the eyes'; to be a very expert thief, to steal the teeth out of one's mouth
adv. By a movement or glance of the eye
or ā-jānā, To get into the head (wine, &c.), to intoxicate; to be intoxicated
āṅkhoṅ-meṅ samānā, To be always before (one's) eyes; to be ever in (one's) mind
To delight the eye; to rejoice; to charm, to fascinate
To be blinded by pride, vanity, lust, &c.; to be wilfully blind, to pretend not to know one
lit. 'To be a thorn in the eye'; to be painful to the eyes, to prove a very unpleasant sight, to be an eye-sore, to be viewed with envy and dislike
(or dhūl) ḍālnā (-kī), To throw dust in the eyes (of); to blind, deceive, cheat, impose upon; to commend or puff wares of an inferior quality; to pilfer or snatch away anything quickly and secretly
āṅkhoṅ-meṅ rāt le-jānā, To pass the night awake, to have restless or sleepless nights
(-kī), To deprive of sight, to blind

āṅkhoṅ-meṅ basnā (-kī), l t. 'To dwell in the eyes' (of); to win the affections (of), possess the heart (of); to have one's image ever before the eyes (cf. āṅkhoṅ-meṅ phirna); — to blind, hoodwink, deceive; to deny (a fact); to persist in one's own erroneous opinions
To appear worthless, or of small account, to be thought little of; to be disapproved of
adv. By a mere movement or glance of the eye; at a glance
To cherish, esteem, &c. ( = āṅkhoṅ-par biṭhānā, q.v.)
To feel the eyes bursting (with pain); to look on astonished till the eyes are ready to burst, to gaze in astonishment; — to be consumed with envy or jealousy

lit. 'Four eyes to meet'; to look into each other's eyes (see ćār āṅkheṅ honā, s.v. ćār); — āṅkheṅ ćhat-se lagnā or lag-jānā, To have the eyes turned upward in anxious expectation; to have the eyes turned upward in death, to be dying
To have a bursting pain in the eyes ( = āṅkheṅ phaṭnā); to have inflamed or sore eyes ( = āṅkh ānā)
To run the eyes round, to look about or around (eagerly or anxiously); to cast wanton glances around

(-kī), To study the inclination, wishes, or temper (of); to behave respectfully (towards); to take example (from), learn (from a better); to be used to good society; to have seen all sorts of society; to be no better than the example set before one
To look one boldly, or shamelessly, in the face
To have a film over the eye, to become blind (with weeping and vain expectation)
(-kī t̤araf), The eyes to be fixed intently (on, or in the direction of), to look out anxiously for
(-kī), To take out the eyes (of), deprive of sight; — to look angrily or menacingly (at), to menace ( = āṅkh aikhāna, and aṅkhānā)
To turn red and blue with rage, become livid with anger, to look very angry
To have eyes, to be able to see (mentally); to get sense or discernment.

def: s.f. The bend or hollow near the knee-pan.
s.m. Sprout, shoot; sprout or shoot in the joint of sugar-cane ( = aṅkhuʼā); offspring, issue, child.
s.f. Valuation, appraisement, estimation, calculation; adjustment of the rents to be paid by the coparceners through the person held liable for the realization of the Government demand.
s.m. The space between two pillars or beams. (The word is, I believe, peculiar to Southern India.)
v.n. To look angry or threatening; to be angry or displeased; to be peevish or fretful.
s.m. = aṅćhar, q.v.
s.m. (sce āṅkh), Valuation, &c.; rough estimate or valuation (without actual measurement) of a standing crop, &c.
s.f. (dim. of āṅkh), Eye; glance of the eye; love-glance. (The plural is ankhiyāṅ).

ety: āṅkh+dim. aff. rī, or ṛī = S. र
def: s.f. (dim. of āṅkh), Eye; glance of the eye.

ety: āṅkh+dim. aff. rī, or ṛī = S. र
def: s.f. (dim. of āṅkh), Eye; glance of the eye.
s.m. = ankurā and āṅkh, q.q.v.
part. Numbered, counted, paged; marked, branded, stamped, ruled (with lines), spotted, stained; approved.
v.n. To be valued, appraised, estimated, weighed, measured, &c. (see next).

v.t. To value, appraise, estimate, weigh, measure, count, reckon; to judge, consider; to mark, stamp, distinguish (a thing) by a mark; to page (a book); — to remove disease or pain by spells and incantations (syn. jhāṛnā).
s.m. A large hook; a kind of grapnel.
v.t. To make crisp; to dry up; to parch; to heat, warm.
s.m. Any iron with a curved or crooked end; hook, tenter; fish-hook; tendril, cirrhus (in this sense aṅkṛī or aṅkrī is the more common).
s.f. Shrinking, contraction; crispness; dryness; cramp.
v.t. To cause to curl up, shrink, or contract (with heat, &c.); to make crisp (with heat); to dry; to heat, to parch (cf. aṅkornā and akṛānā).
= aṅkṛānā, q.v.
s.f. A kind of vetch, Vicia sativa; — wet grain; — bramble (see s.v. āṅćū); — hook, &c. = āṅkṛī, q.v.
= H آنکڙي आंकड़ी āṅkṛī, s.f. dim. of āṅkṛā, q.v.
= H انکڙي अंकड़ी aṅkṛī, s.f. dim. of āṅkṛā, q.v.
s.f. A young sprout.
s.m. One who values, &c.; appraiser ( = ankak); an officer employed to survey and appraise standing crops.
s.m. Sprout, shoot, blade, seed-bud, germ.
= H انکورا अंकूरा aṅkūrā, = aṅkur, aṅkurā, q.q.v.
s.m. Sprout, shoot, blade, seed-bud, germ.
= H انکور अंकूर aṅkūr, = aṅkur, aṅkurā, q.q.v.
s.m. The iron hook with which elephants are driven; goad; anything or person that acts as a spur or impels to action, &c.
subs: v.t. To goad; to reduce to submission, bring to obedience.

def: s.m. The iron hook with which elephants are driven; goad; anything or person that acts as a spur or impels to action, &c.
subs: v.t. To goad; to reduce to submission, bring to obedience.

def: s.f. (dim. of aṅkus, q.v.), Hook, tenter.
s.m. = aṅkak and āṅkū, q.q.v.
v.t. To cause to value, &c.; also = aṅkānā, q.v.
s.f. Embrace; bosom
subs: To take into, or to fill, the embrace; to embrace.

def: adj. Out of sorts, indisposed; dejected, sad, agitated, troubled in mind; dull.
s.f. Agitation, distress of mind, perplexity, doubt.
v.n. To be dejected, perplexed, agitated, troubled in mind, &c. (see anmanā).

adj. Unmixed, without alloy, unadulterated, pure, genuine, disconnected, not related; not agreeing or harmonizing, discordant, ill-assorted, unsuitable, jarring; heterogeneous; out of proportion, disproportioned; unseasonable; foreign (to)
adj. Ill-assorted, unsuitable, &c. = an-mel, q.v.; — s.f. A kind of composition in which ludicrous and absurd combinations are presented in a humorous light; ludicrous misjoinders.
adj. Priceless, invaluable; very valuable; precious.
adj. Having the face turned away or averted; — adv. With the face averted.

ety: S. अन्न+दा; cf. Zend ana, an, and T. anā, 'mother'
def: s.f. A wet-nurse, nurse (syn. dhāʼī, dāʼī).
v.t. = آننا ānnā, q.v.
wety: Inherited from Prakrit 𑀆𑀯𑁂𑀇 (āvei), from Sanskrit आपयति (āpayati), from आप् (āp) + -अयति (-ayati). The perfective form آیا (āyā) may be derived from Sanskrit आगत (ā́gata, “arrived”).

def: s.f. Wet-nurse. See انا annā.
v.n. To come = ānā, q.v.
v.t. To bring = lānā.
s.m. Food, nourishment, victuals, bread; grain, corn; boiled rice
subs: adj. & s.m. Eating food; — food-eater; rice-eater
(S. anna-prāśana), s.m. The ceremony of making a child taste rice boiled in milk (or khīr) for the first time ( = khīr ćaṭānā) — which is usually done between the fifth and eighth month)
s.f. Satiety; — name of a goddess, the Ceres of Hindū mythology
s.m. Food and water, victuals; subsistence, maintenance; — ann-jal karnā, ann-jal-pān karnā, v.n. To eat and drink, to take food, make a meal
s.m. Food-giver, provider, supporter; benefactor, patron; employer, master, lord
s.m. Grain and wealth, wealth both in grain and other property; corn and cattle; affluence, competency
s.m. lit. 'Grain-worm'; (met.) man. cf. anāj-kā kīṛā

an-kuṭ, s.m. A festival celebrated by the Hindūs (in commemoration of Krishna's sacrifice) on the day following the Dīwālī by the offering of a large quantity of mixed meats in a temple dedicated to Vishnu. (A morsel of this mess is given by the officiating priest to each worshipper, who finds therein some special article of food from which he or she had vowed temporarily to abstain in the hope of obtaining some desired blessing)
v.n. To derive no pleasure from food, to be indifferent, or averse, to food
adj. Destitute of food, or of subsistence; needy.
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀅𑀡- (aṇa-), from Sanskrit अन्- (an-), from Proto-Indo-European *n̥- (“not, un-”).

def: s.f. Wet-nurse = annā, q.v.
s.f. See انني annī.
s.f. A one-anna piece = ik-annī.
s.m. See s.v. ann.
v.s.v. ann.
v.s.v. ann.
adj. (f. -ī), Uncommon, rare, wonderful, extraordinary, unprecedented; unique, singular, peculiar, novel, new, strange ( = anūṭhā); low, base, mean.
s.m. Purified sulphur
adj. Without salt, saltless ( = a-lonā, q.v.).
s.f. Indigestion ( = bad-haẓmī), morbid weakness of the stomach.
adj. (f. -ī), Unobtainable.
adj. Unlettered, uneducated, uninformed, illiterate.
adj. Unlettered, uneducated, uninformed, illiterate.
adj. Unfriendly; faithless, unbelieving.
s.m. Testicle.
part. adj. Unguarded, unprotected, uncared for.
s.m. Want of flavour or enjoyment; distaste; disgust; nausea; coolness, misunderstanding between friends.
adj. Free from debt, undebted.
adj. Untrue, false.

adj. Contrary to custom, rule or practice, not en règle, not parliamentary; contrary to good manners or breeding, unmannerly, ill-behaved; — s.f. Irregularity; unmannerliness, ill-behaviour, unceremoniousness ( = ku-rīt).
s.m. Part, portion share division; degree of lat. or long.). degree, grade (of a circle = darja); numerator of a fraction; right, possession. (For compounds see anś.)
s.f. (?) = H انس अंस aṅs, s.m. [S. अंशु, q.v.], The fibrous part of anything; fibre, pith, marrow, essence, substance; strength, vigour, energy, stamina; semen.
s.m. Part, portion, share, &c. = ans, q.v.
subs: (aṅśa+aṅśa), s.m. Share of a share, subdivision
s.m. A deed setting forth the shares of a property between the members of a Hindū family, a deed of partition ( = aṅśī)
aṅś-dhārī, adj. Sharing, partaking; successful; (in Theology) becoming incarnate; — s.m. Sharer, partaker; incarnation
(anśa+vaṅśa+ka), s.m. A measure of land (equal to one-twentieth of a pilwāṅsā)
s.m. One who takes a share of an estate, joint sharer, co-heir; partner.

def: s.f. (?) [S. अंशु, q.v.], The fibrous part of anything; fibre, pith, marrow, essence, substance; strength, vigour, energy, stamina; semen.
adj. compar. & superl. (of munāsi, q.v.), More, or most, fitting or suitable; more, or most, convenient; very proper, very advisable.
s.m. pl. (of نسب nasab), Genealogies; races; families, generations.
adj. Without understanding or sense; ignorant, stupid; thoughtless, heedless; inexperienced.
part. adj. Not understood; not understanding or knowing. (See an-samajh.)
s.m. pl. (of nāṣir), Helpers, assistants, friends (especially of Moḥammad); the Arabs who attended Moḥammad on his departure from Mecca to Medina.
adj. Pertaining to, or of, the Anṣār; — s.f. A tribe of Moḥammadan Shaiḵẖs, supposed to have come originally from Medina.
adj. = anś-dhārī, q.v.s.v. aṅś.
s.m. Sharer, &c., see aṅśī.
s.f. Share, &c. = anś, q.v.
subs: (-kī), To divide or distribute in shares or portions.

def: s.m. Sharer, participator, partner, share-holder, co-heir; — a deed setting forth the shares or divisions of a property; — a deed of partition between the members of a Hindī family.
s.m. = aṅśik, q.v.
s.m. Sharer, participator, partner, co-heir, proprietor.
adj. Unlearned, untaught, illiterate.
adj. Unlearned, untaught, illiterate.
= an-sikhā, q.v.
adj. Thoughtless, inattentive, &c.
s.m. Tear, tears
subs: v.n. lit. 'Tears to come'; to have tears in the eyes, to weep
To make tears flow; to shed tears; to pretend to weep
To have the eyes filled with tears, to weep
āṅsū ṭapak-paṛnā, v.n. lit. 'Tears to flow, or to drop'; to weep
v.n. To have (one's) tears wiped away; to be comforted or soothed
(-ke), To wipe away the tears (of), dry (one's) tears; to console, comfort, soothe
āṅsū ḍhalak, s.f. Running or watering of the eyes (a disease in horses, supposed to arise from a coil of hair in the outer corner of the eye)
lit. 'To wash the face with tears'; to shed tears copiously = āṭh āṭh āṅsū ronā, q.v.s.v. āṭh.
ex: اُس کی آن٘کھوں میں آن٘سُو بَھر آئے۔
us kī ā̃khõ mẽ ā̃sū bhar āe.
Her eyes were filled with tears.
wety: Inherited from Prakrit 𑀅𑀁𑀲𑀼 (aṃsu), from Sanskrit अश्रु॑ (áśru), from Proto-Indo-Iranian *Háćru, from Proto-Indo-European *h₂eḱru, from *h₂eḱ-. Cognates include Gujarati આંસુ (ā̃su), Marathi आसू (āsū), Nepali आँसु (ā̃su), Punjabi ہنجھو / ਹੰਝੂ (hañjhū), and Sindhi هَنجَ (hañja).

def: adj. Turning a deaf ear to, disregarding, unmindful, heedless; — disregarded, &c. = next, q.v.
part. adj. (f. -ī), Unheard, turned a deaf ear to, unheeded, disregarded
subs: v.t. To turn a deaf ear to, to disregard, to treat as not heard, to pretend not to hear. (The phrase is generally preceded by the word sunā, e.g. sunā ansunā karnā.)

def: s.m. See s.v. aṅś.

s.m. End, limit, term, boundary, border; period, utmost extent, whole amount; completion, conclusion, termination; issue, result, consequence; end of life, death, dissolution, destruction; end in view, aim, intention, purpose; definite ascertainment, certain knowledge, certainty; knowledge or intelligence (of), account (of), news; inner part, inside, life, soul, mind, heart; nature, essence; condition; proximity; — adj. Last, final, ultimate; — adv. (S. ante), In the end, at last, finally, after all ( = ant-ko and ant-meṅ)
subs: s.m. The last or latter part, the end; the New Testament (in contradistinction to ādi-bhāg or the Old Testament)
s.m. The hour of death, dying moment, the end
adv. At last, finally, in the end, ultimately
s.m. (in Prosody), A metre that has a short or light final syllable
s.m. (in Prosody), Any measure having a long or heavy final syllable
(-kā), To worry the life out (of), to harass, plague, persecute

def: s.f. Entrails, intestines, guts: viscera, tripe
subs: v.n. To suffer from the coming down of the rectum
Protrusion of a gut, rupture, hernia
v.n. To void white glutinous stools
lit. 'To double or fold up the guts'; to go without food

def: s.f. Twist, turn, fold; a peculiar fold of the dhotī (q.v.); entanglement; knot, joint (of a finger, &c. cf. aṅṭī, āṅtī); mark or scratch made on silver or gold to test its purity; opposition, contrariety, antagonism, enmity; grudge; envy; jar; misunderstanding (cf. ainṭ, anṭī)
subs: (-meṅ), To become knotted or entangled; to have a feeling of antagonism, enmity, envy, &c., to be at enmity (with), have a grudge (against); to have a misunderstanding or difference (with)
anṭ-kī sanṭ, adj. Irrelevant, unsuitable, inapplicable, inappropriate
s.f. Combination, league, intrigue, plot; a rambling or irrelevant speech; an evasive answer.

ety: cf. āṅṭhī, anṭī, and perhaps anḍā
def: s.m. Ball, marble pill (as of opium); a large shell or kauṛī
subs: adj. Pledging the shell with which he plays (a gambler); — s.m. The shell played with and pledged (by a gambler when he has lost all his money)
(ćit = S. क्षिप्त), adj. lit. 'Lying like a ball,' or 'the ball or shell thrown'; prostrate, fallen; senseless, insensible; dead drunk; — reduced to the last extremity; unlucky
v.n. To be prostrate; to fall down; to be dead drunk; — to be reduced to the last extremity, to be very unlucky

def: adj. Choke full; restricted, confined; scanty, insufficient; confining closely, restraining; restrictive, severe.
adj. Interior, internal, inner, &c. (used as first member of compounds)
subs: s.m. A royal palace; the inner, or women's apartments

anta-stha, s.m. lit. 'Situated in the interior or middle'; the semi-vowels य र, ल, व (so called because, in the arrangement of the Devanāgarī alphabet, these letters stand between the other consonants and the sibilants)
s.m. The inner or spiritual part (of a man), soul, conscience
anta-karaṇ, antas-karaṇ, antash-karaṇ, s.m. Understanding, mind, soul, conscience, heart; that which is in the mind, thoughts, will, intention, motive
s.m. Inner corner; interior angle ( = antar-koṇ).

def: s.m. A Śūdra; a person of low caste.
